description: The Forty-Two Gang was a teenage street gang in Chicago active from 1925 to 1934, founded by Italian-American youths in the 'Patch' and 'Little Hell' neighborhoods. It served as a recruitment pool for the Chicago Outfit, producing future mobsters like Sam Giancana. The gang engaged in vandalism, theft, burglary, and armed robbery, and was known for extreme violence. It disbanded after heavy losses from rival wars and arrests.
